member.name: Heidi Alexander
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Heidi Alexander is the Labour MP for Swindon South , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. She currently holds the Government post of Secretary of State for Transport.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Crime Prevention: Glasgow
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the effectiveness of Police Scotland’s violence reduction unit in Glasgow, and whether she has plans to replicate the work of that unit in England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have not made an assessment of the effectiveness of the Violence Reduction Unit in Scotland. We will shortly be publishing our new Serious Violence Strategy which will respond to concerns about serious violence especially recent increases in knife crim...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Labour Mobility
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Exiting the European Union, with reference to the Prime Ministers speech of 2 March 2018 on the UK’s future economic partnership with the EU, what the definition is of self-employed professionals in relation to future la
writtenQuestions.answerText: Freedom of movement is ending but there will continue to be migration and mobility between the UK and the EU after the UK leaves. The Prime Minister has made it clear that the Government is open to discussing with the EU how to facilitate the valuable lin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Exiting the European Union

writtenQuestions.heading: EU Institutions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Exiting the European Union, with reference to the Prime Minister's speech of 2 March 2018 on the UK’s future economic partnership with the EU, whether the list of pan-European regulatory agencies to which the UK will be s
writtenQuestions.answerText: As the Prime Minister said in her speech in Mansion House on 2 March 2018, we will want to explore with the EU the terms on which we could remain part of EU agencies such as those that are critical for the chemicals, medicines and aerospace industries: th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Exiting the European Union

writtenQuestions.heading: Southeastern: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether the Rail Accident Investigations Branch will be involved in the independent investigation into the response of Southeastern Trains and Network Rail to the self-evacuation of passengers on trains outside
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Rail Accident Investigations Branch (RAIB) are aware of the incident at Lewisham and the understandable public concern related to it. This has led to them to start what they refer to as a “preliminary examination” into the event. They are currently ga...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Railway Stations: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the oral contribution of 5 February 2018, Official Report, column 1248 on railway station accessibility, when he plans to make a decision on the funding to be allocated to station accessibility impr
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Statement of Funds Available, we expect around £47.9bn to be spent on the railway between 2019 and 2024, with up to £34.7bn to be provided directly via Government grant. This will include funding to improve accessibility across the netwo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Support for Mortgage Interest
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how many people have applied to convert Support for Mortgage Interest payments into a loan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All existing support for mortgage interest claimants will be contacted and offered the new support for mortgage interest loan, there is no need for them to apply. A managed transfer process is underway, with all existing recipients of support for mortgage...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Sexually Transmitted Infections: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether there is a requirement for online sellers of testing kits for sexually transmitted infections to be registered with the Care Quality Commission.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There is no requirement for online sellers of testing kits for sexually transmitted infections to be registered with the Care Quality Commission (CQC) unless the provider is registered with the CQC as a primary care service.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Sexually Transmitted Infections: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what duties online sexually transmitted infection testing services have to follow best practice in respect of safeguarding and child protection issues.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Online sexually transmitted infection services are not offered to under 16s who would be advised to attend their local clinic where a proper safeguarding assessment can be undertaken in person.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Cycling: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when he plans to commence the second phase of the review of cycle safety announced on 21 September, into wider road safety issues.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department expects to publish a Call for Evidence early in 2018. Preliminary discussions regarding Phase Two have already started.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Rare Diseases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health, whether the UK hospitals participating in the European Reference Networks on rare diseases will continue to participate in those Networks in the event of the UK withdrawing from the EU.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government's policy paper, Collaboration on Science and Innovation: A Future Partnership Paper, stresses the importance of continued collaboration with European partners to ensure that the United Kingdom remains one of the best places in the world for...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: As indicated in the National Audit Office report, the Department for Exiting the European Union had identified 313 Exit work streams across Government departments, including 31 from this Department. The development of work streams is part of ongoing cross...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Exiting the European Union: Brexit

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Exiting the European Union is working closely with all departments to ensure our preparations for exit from and new partnership with the EU are on track. The Department has established a series of arrangements to coordinate departments’...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Exiting the European Union

writtenQuestions.heading: Migrants
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many applications for limited leave to remain were granted with no recourse to public funds in each year from 2010 to 2016.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I am sorry but this information is not held in the format requested and could only be obtained at disproportionate cost.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office
